essence: Installs, maintains, and repairs overhead and underground electrical distribution networks that power homes and businesses.

identity.title: Electricity Network Lineworker
identity.summary: Works on overhead lines, poles, substations, and underground cables to ensure safe and reliable electricity distribution.

salary.notes: High‑voltage specialists, live‑line workers, and emergency response teams can exceed £60k-£75k+.

qualifications: Electrical network training (e.g., DNO apprenticeship or linesperson certification)
Knowledge of electrical safety, switching, and isolation procedures
Understanding of overhead line construction and underground cable systems

lifestyle.description: Electricity network lineworkers maintain and repair the infrastructure that keeps the power grid running. Days involve climbing poles, working at height, handling high‑voltage equipment, repairing storm damage, installing new lines, and performing safety‑critical switching operations. The role is physical, technical, and often involves emergency callouts, especially during severe weather. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy outdoor work, teamwork, and high‑responsibility technical tasks.

habits: Performs careful equipment and PPE checks
Communicates clearly with control rooms and team members
Keeps tools and climbing gear organised
Maintains accurate records of work and inspections
